The '''Parliament of Moralan''' was the ruling body of the [[Moralan (species)|Moralan]] [[Species/Legends|species]]' [[Moralan republic|republic]] which was founded after the species freed themselves from [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] to the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] species.<ref name="EA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The species freed themselves with encouragement from the [[Tionese (group)/Legends|Tionese]], enemies of the Hutts from the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]], and the parliament had been formed by the time of [[Second Battle of Vontor]] during the [[Xim Wars|Hutt–Xim conflict]], a war between the Tionese and the Hutts. At the time of the battle the Tionese had forces present in the [[Moralan system/Legends|Moralan system]] where [[Moralan]], the Moralan [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], was located but shortly afterwards they withdrew. With the Tionese gonem a Hutt named [[Boonta Hestilic Shad'ruu/Legends|Boonta Hestilic Shad'ruu]] [[Invasion of Moralan|attacked]] the republic and conquered it, driving the Moralans to extinction and so destroying the parliament.<ref name="Xim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-the-history-of-xim-and-the-tion-cluster|text=Essential Atlas Extra: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster|int=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster}}</ref> The battle lasted three days and was celebrated in the three-day holiday collectively known as [[Boonta Eve/Legends|Boonta's Eve]], named after the hero of the battle.<ref name="droids">{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The parliament of Moralan was first mentioned in an article by [[Rich Handley]] and [[Abel G. Peña]] on the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars Droids'']] TV series in the [[Polyhedron 170|170th]] issue of the ''[[Polyhedron]]'' role-playing magazine released in [[June]] [[2004]]. [[Daniel Wallace]] stated in the endnotes for the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' that the Moralan species he and [[Jason Fry]] mentioned were a reference to the parliament.<ref name="End">{{Blog|danwallace|144|Endnotes for Star Wars: The Essential Atlas (part 2 of 5)}}</ref> Fry mentioned them again in the reference book ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' written by him and [[Paul R. Urquhart]] in [[2012]].
